title: RunParameterInsights
description: CLIENT-SAFE parameter-importance analysis for one optimization run targeting a single measure, used by the terminal-first analytics surface to answer 'which knobs mattered and what should I do with the config space next?'. Reports the target measure, the minimum-trials reliability floor, the ranked drivers (importance score, confidence interval, best value, and an expand/freeze/narrow suggested action), and notable parameter interactions. Importance scores are derived from the run's measured aggregates; this contract carries no proprietary example-scoring signals or formulas.

run_id.description: Identifier of the optimization run this analysis describes.

target_measure.description: Name of the measure whose drivers are being explained.

min_trials.description: Minimum trial count a parameter needed before its importance is reported as reliable.

drivers.description: Tuned parameters ranked by how much they drive the target measure.

importance_score.description: Importance of this parameter for the target measure (higher = more influential).

confidence_interval.description: Two-element [lower, upper] confidence interval for the importance score.

best_value.description: Best observed value for this parameter (type matches the parameter's domain: string, number, boolean, or null).

suggested_action.description: Coarse next action for this parameter's search range.

interactions.description: Notable interactions between pairs of parameters.

effect.description: Signed strength of the interaction effect.

note.description: Templated, client-safe description of the interaction.

warnings.description: Client-safe caveats about the analysis (e.g. too few trials, confounded parameters).
